title: Get A Job

jokes.text: If you think you can do it any faster, then you come in here.
jokes.setup: Someone complaining Will is taking too long
jokes.punchline: Will's sassy comeback challenging them to do better
jokes.explanation: Standard Will attitude - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Hey, look, I just ain't trying to be out there looking stupid, all right?
jokes.setup: Will concerned about looking stupid
jokes.punchline: The irony that he's already in a chicken suit
jokes.explanation: Good ironic setup - Will's vanity vs. his current situation

jokes.text: Hold up, Carlton. Wait a minute. A chicken eating chicken? That's just sick.
jokes.setup: Carlton in chicken suit doing chicken commercial
jokes.punchline: Will pointing out the cannibalistic absurdity
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about the commercial's logical flaw

jokes.text: What part of the chicken you like?
jokes.setup: Will distracted from work by attractive woman
jokes.punchline: Using chicken commercial as pickup line
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will move - turning any situation into flirtation

jokes.text: Break it up. You, out. Big Bird, back to work.
jokes.setup: Will in chicken suit being told to get back to work
jokes.punchline: Calling him Big Bird
jokes.explanation: Simple visual reference joke, functional but not inspired

jokes.text: Because if you don't, I'll fire you from the Peacock. You'll drop out of school and be wrestling for scraps... with a guy named Joe in the dumpster behind the Sizzler.
jokes.setup: Will complaining about the job
jokes.punchline: Carlton's overly specific doomsday scenario
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with specific absurd details that feel Carlton-authentic

jokes.text: But, hey, you have to live your own life.
jokes.setup: Carlton's dramatic threat about Will's future
jokes.punchline: Casual, dismissive follow-up as if he didn't just paint a dire picture
jokes.explanation: Excellent comedic timing and structure - the casual dismissal after intense threat is perfectly executed

jokes.text: Hi. As the ain't-got-no-damn-choice king of poultry...
jokes.setup: Will forced to do commercial despite complaints
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive modification of the script
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will rebellion - subverting the script while technically complying

jokes.text: I am not experiencing happy chicken here.
jokes.setup: Will clearly unhappy in chicken suit
jokes.punchline: Director's understated professional response
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of obvious situation

jokes.text: With all due respect, Will, you're not exactly overqualified.
jokes.setup: Will complaining about doing stupid jobs
jokes.punchline: Carlton's polite but cutting assessment of Will's qualifications
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton delivery - polite phrasing that delivers a sharp insult. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Read my beak: I quit.
jokes.setup: Will quitting while still in chicken suit
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'read my lips' while wearing beak
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works perfectly with the visual situation

jokes.text: It's him. He started it.
jokes.setup: Adult workplace conflict
jokes.punchline: Both grown men reverting to childish blame-pointing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing their cousin-brother dynamic, though concept is familiar

jokes.text: Hey, I mean, I can assist. I'm talented. Definitely coordinated.
jokes.setup: Job for 'assistant talent coordinator'
jokes.punchline: Will breaking down the job title literally
jokes.explanation: Typical Will confidence, functional wordplay

jokes.text: What are you doing here, Teeny? You already got a good job.
jokes.setup: Carlton appearing to compete for the same job
jokes.punchline: Will's height-based nickname for Carlton
jokes.explanation: Standard Will insult, relies on physical comedy crutch

jokes.text: Hilary rapid-fire hiring both of them for the same job
jokes.setup: Both guys wanting the job
jokes.punchline: Hilary's airheaded solution of giving it to both
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - solving conflict with illogical solution

jokes.text: I can type 100 words a minute, I take shorthand, speak French and Spanish. I'm pre-law with a 4.0 average. And you? Well, you know, I mean, I don't like to brag... But? Oh, that's it. I just don't like to brag.
jokes.setup: Carlton listing impressive qualifications, asking Will for his
jokes.punchline: Will's non-qualification presented as modesty
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/punchline structure with perfect character contrast

jokes.text: Pay no attention to him. He's a chicken.
jokes.setup: Will still in chicken suit trying to compete
jokes.punchline: Carlton dismissing him literally and figuratively
jokes.explanation: Clever double meaning - literal chicken costume, figurative coward

jokes.text: That's right, you've got it. Ha, ha. He's a follower not a leader.
jokes.setup: Will pointing out Carlton made him wear the costume
jokes.punchline: Carlton spinning this as proof Will lacks leadership
jokes.explanation: Clever Carlton moment showing his ability to spin any situation to his advantage

jokes.text: You know, you remind me of myself. I used to be an entry-level butt-kisser too.
jokes.setup: Carlton being overly eager to impress
jokes.punchline: Werner recognizing the type and being blunt about it
jokes.explanation: Good character observation, cynical industry humor

jokes.text: That big Blow Pop head swindled his way in and took what was mine.
jokes.setup: Will complaining about Carlton getting the job
jokes.punchline: Creative insult about Carlton's head shape
jokes.explanation: Creative visual insult, though relies on physical comedy

jokes.text: Damn. That's even worse than getting beat up by a girl.
jokes.setup: Will losing job to Carlton
jokes.punchline: Jazz's assessment of how embarrassing this is
jokes.explanation: Functional escalation but relies on dated gender stereotype

jokes.text: Come get your freak on at Club Freak On. Took me a long time to find something that rhymed with Freak On.
jokes.setup: Jazz showing club flyer
jokes.punchline: Admitting the obvious rhyme took effort
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of obvious failure

jokes.text: MC Steal-a-Job is in the house.
jokes.setup: Carlton arriving after taking Will's job
jokes.punchline: Will's rapper-style nickname for Carlton
jokes.explanation: Clever combination of hip-hop culture and situation

jokes.text: Do I detect a little hostility, Will? Sour grapes perchance?
jokes.setup: Will's obvious anger at Carlton
jokes.punchline: Carlton's pompous, formal language ('perchance') to describe Will's frustration
jokes.explanation: Good Carlton character moment showing his pretentious vocabulary and smugness

jokes.text: Pay no attention to my unemployed friend here.
jokes.setup: Carlton dismissing Will to important guest
jokes.punchline: Emphasizing Will's unemployment status
jokes.explanation: Standard put-down, functional but not creative

jokes.text: Oh, two of y'all can fit on a Big Wheel?
jokes.setup: Carlton mentioning escorting Maurice's sister
jokes.punchline: Will's height joke about Carlton and the sister
jokes.explanation: Clever visual joke combining height and childish toy reference

jokes.text: That's it. Beak on. 'Come get your beak on at...'
jokes.setup: Carlton's beak comment and Jazz's earlier club name
jokes.punchline: Will combining both into new club name parody
jokes.explanation: Creative callback combining multiple elements, shows Will's quick wit

jokes.text: You were funnier with your beak on. That's it. Beak on. Come get your beak on at...
jokes.setup: Carlton insulting Will's humor
jokes.punchline: Will adapting the club name to reference chicken suit
jokes.explanation: Good callback combining multiple earlier elements

jokes.text: Then this Chinese guy comes in and says, 'Supplies. Supplies.'
jokes.setup: Maurice telling a story/joke
jokes.punchline: Play on 'supplies/surprise' with accent stereotype
jokes.explanation: Dated ethnic stereotype humor that relies on accent mockery rather than clever writing

jokes.text: Then this Chinese guy comes in and says, 'Supplies. Surprises.' Oh, that is so funny. I don't get it, but you are so funny.
jokes.setup: Maurice telling what appears to be a bad joke
jokes.punchline: Hilary's reaction admitting she doesn't understand but laughing anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary - fake laughing at what she doesn't understand

jokes.text: Excuse me, Bonbon? We're not finished talking yet.
jokes.setup: Carlton using French phrase 'bon mots'
jokes.punchline: Will's mispronunciation as candy name
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Will's street smarts vs. Carlton's pretension

jokes.text: Although we never really did a background check.
jokes.setup: Carlton introducing Will as his cousin
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Will might not actually be family
jokes.explanation: Good character-consistent dig at Will's outsider status in the family

jokes.text: Why don't you go back into the kitchen and play with your future cell mate?
jokes.setup: Carlton dismissing Will from business meeting
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Will and Jazz are destined for prison
jokes.explanation: Carlton trying to be cutting but relies on classist assumptions

jokes.text: Oh, speaking of schedules, Carlton. The clinic called. You're still on for your implant tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to sound professional about schedules
jokes.punchline: Will's fake interruption about medical procedure
jokes.explanation: Good sabotage joke that fits Will's character

jokes.text: Oh, nothing but the rotting residue of the lies you told to steal my job.
jokes.setup: Carlton asking what's on his lip
jokes.punchline: Will's overly elaborate poetic insult
jokes.explanation: Creative escalation with poetic language that's funny in context

jokes.text: Yeah? Well, the bigger, badder man's about to beat the better man into oblivion.
jokes.setup: Carlton claiming to be the better man
jokes.punchline: Will's alliterative threat response
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with alliteration that escalates the verbal confrontation

jokes.text: You think it's easy catering to a low-rent Martin Lawrence wannabe?
jokes.setup: Will ranting about Carlton's job qualifications
jokes.punchline: Insulting Maurice (who's sitting right there) as second-rate comedian
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Maurice's reaction, though insult is a bit cheap

jokes.text: I don't have to sit here and be insulted... by some black Potsie Weber. I'm out of here, okay?
jokes.setup: Will's insults about Maurice
jokes.punchline: Maurice comparing Carlton to nerdy Happy Days character
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback using apt TV reference for Carlton's character type

jokes.text: Pardon me. I don't have to sit here and be insulted by some black Potsie Weber. I'm out of here, okay?
jokes.setup: Maurice responding to Will's insult
jokes.punchline: Calling Carlton a Black version of Happy Days nerd character
jokes.explanation: Perfect pop culture reference that nails Carlton's persona

jokes.text: You kind of drifted into a no-fly zone. Look, Potsie don't know what he talking about, all right?
jokes.setup: Will trying to salvage situation with Maurice
jokes.punchline: Adopting Maurice's 'Potsie' nickname for Carlton
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Will adapting to save the situation

jokes.text: Look, Potsie don't know what he talking about, all right?
jokes.setup: Maurice's Potsie Weber comparison
jokes.punchline: Will adopting the nickname to dismiss Carlton
jokes.explanation: Good callback usage that shows Will embracing the insult to win Maurice back

jokes.text: But keep the Smurf away from me. That's doable. You're fired. Will, you're hired.
jokes.setup: Maurice agreeing to do the show but with conditions
jokes.punchline: Another height-based nickname and immediate personnel change
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and resolution, though height jokes are repetitive

jokes.text: You know, it's true what they say, TV really does add 10 pounds. Hmm. What would account for the other 250?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil on TV looking heavy
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's cutting math about his weight
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey moment - cutting wit with mathematical precision

jokes.text: You know, you're not a man who enjoys employment, are you, Geoffrey?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey's insult about Phil's weight
jokes.punchline: Phil's threat disguised as observation
jokes.explanation: Good comeback that maintains Phil's authority while being witty

jokes.text: Food? Ha-ha-ha. Shut up.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey suggesting they get food after weight jokes
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's embarrassed reaction and order to shut up
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction to food suggestion after weight jokes, predictable but character-appropriate

jokes.text: Studying, huh? What is this, your geometry dress?
jokes.setup: Ashley dressed up claiming she was studying
jokes.punchline: Will's comment about the dress being too revealing/geometric
jokes.explanation: Good big brother moment with clever wordplay about revealing dress vs. studying

jokes.text: Well, if it isn't the wise-cracking, job-taking, sister-stealing slug himself.
jokes.setup: Carlton encountering Will after losing the job
jokes.punchline: String of alliterative accusations ending with 'slug'
jokes.explanation: Good rhythm and alliteration in Carlton's bitter greeting, shows his wounded pride

jokes.text: Whoo! Who's sucking on them sour grapes now, huh, Carlton?
jokes.setup: Will got Carlton's job back
jokes.punchline: Referencing Carlton's earlier 'sour grapes' comment
jokes.explanation: Standard callback structure, effective but predictable

jokes.text: Now, which one of you handsome men is Big Willy?
jokes.setup: Jasmine arriving to meet Will for their date
jokes.punchline: Her appearance doesn't match Will's expectations
jokes.explanation: Relies on appearance-based comedy which is somewhat cheap

jokes.text: Will's stunned reaction and long pause
jokes.setup: Jasmine's appearance
jokes.punchline: Will's visible shock and speechlessness
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction beat, relies on appearance comedy

jokes.text: Looks like you already got it on.
jokes.setup: Jasmine saying she's ready to 'get her freak on'
jokes.punchline: Will commenting on her appearance/outfit
jokes.explanation: Cheap shot at appearance, below Will's usual wit level

jokes.text: Oh, no, it's my stomach. I probably should've waited about an hour before we danced.
jokes.setup: Will appearing to be in pain/discomfort
jokes.punchline: Blaming it on eating before dancing (classic excuse)
jokes.explanation: Classic excuse to escape awkward situation, functional but not inspired

jokes.text: Oh, I put her back under her bridge.
jokes.setup: Jazz asking where the girl is
jokes.punchline: Will's troll reference about Jasmine
jokes.explanation: Clever reference but mean-spirited appearance-based humor

jokes.text: Why don't you hurry and write your number down before I don't want it no more?
jokes.setup: Will trying to get a woman's number
jokes.punchline: Creating false urgency about his own interest
jokes.explanation: Creative pickup line with good psychological pressure tactic, very Will-like

jokes.text: You are learning entirely too much from me, young lady.
jokes.setup: Ashley caught sneaking out to club
jokes.punchline: Will recognizing his bad influence on her
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment showing Will's growth and responsibility

jokes.text: You said, 'Jasmine,' I thought you said, 'Jazz, man.'
jokes.setup: Hilary asking about Jasmine
jokes.punchline: Will's wordplay excuse about the similar sounds
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits Will's quick-thinking character

jokes.text: First time I have a chance to prove that I'm not a joke... and I am.
jokes.setup: Will reflecting on messing up important opportunity
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission of his own failure
jokes.explanation: Excellent character development moment - Will's rare moment of vulnerability

jokes.text: First time I have a chance to prove that I'm not a joke... and I am.
jokes.setup: Will feeling bad about messing up Jasmine situation
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission of being exactly what people think
jokes.explanation: Excellent character development moment with perfect economy and genuine emotion

jokes.text: You asked if she called, sir. She was here.
jokes.setup: Will asking if Jasmine called, upset she's missing
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's literal interpretation revealing she visited
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey - technically correct while being unhelpful

jokes.text: You asked if she called, sir.
jokes.setup: Will asking if Jasmine called, Geoffrey saying no, then revealing she visited
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's overly literal interpretation of the question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey character moment showing his pedantic nature and subtle sabotage

jokes.text: Don't know, don't care.
jokes.setup: Will asking why Carlton took Jasmine
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's complete indifference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey delivery - maximum sass in minimum words

jokes.text: Will a week cover it? Better make it two. Two's good.
jokes.setup: Phil catching Ashley lying about studying
jokes.punchline: Negotiating her punishment like a business deal
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phil's lawyer instincts even in parenting

jokes.text: You know, how we can live together all these years, almost like brothers... and you could still low-bridge me the way you did.
jokes.setup: Will confronting Carlton about stealing his girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Using 'low-bridge' (wrestling term) while being genuinely hurt
jokes.explanation: Good blend of street language with genuine emotion

jokes.text: Snake? Weasel? Oh, yes. Nerd? Oh, yes. How about square-headed short stack?
jokes.setup: Will calling Carlton names
jokes.punchline: Carlton agreeing with insults until Will gets more specific/creative
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and list format with specific physical description that breaks Carlton's agreement pattern

jokes.text: This is not the way this is supposed to be going.
jokes.setup: Carlton expecting Will to be more upset about the job theft
jokes.punchline: Carlton's confusion that Will isn't reacting as expected
jokes.explanation: Good meta-moment showing Carlton's calculation and confusion when his plan backfires

jokes.text: Who may not be a looker, but is quite an entertaining gal.
jokes.setup: Carlton defending his actions with Jasmine
jokes.punchline: Damning with faint praise about her appearance
jokes.explanation: Typical Carlton diplomacy but relies on appearance-based humor

jokes.text: You think you're just gonna charm your way through. Just like your father.
jokes.setup: Carlton criticizing Will's work ethic
jokes.punchline: Comparing Will to his absent father
jokes.explanation: Powerful moment that cuts deep - Carlton using Will's biggest weakness

jokes.text: Let me tell you something. I am nothing like my father, all right?
jokes.setup: Carlton's cutting comparison to Will's father
jokes.punchline: Will's defensive response showing he's hit a nerve
jokes.explanation: Powerful character moment showing Will's vulnerability

jokes.text: And besides, the Ivy League looks down on the entertainment industry.
jokes.setup: Carlton explaining why he gave up the entertainment job
jokes.punchline: His snobbish concern about academic reputation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character moment showing his academic snobbery and priorities

jokes.text: There's some hundred percent USDA prime choice down there. I could be tenderizing right now. Tenderizing as we speak.
jokes.setup: Will talking about missing out at the club
jokes.punchline: Using meat grading/preparation as sexual metaphor
jokes.explanation: Creative extended metaphor that's very Will-esque

jokes.text: Carlton was right. It was only a three-and-a-half-hour virus.
jokes.setup: Will thought he successfully ditched Jasmine
jokes.punchline: Revealing she was actually sick and is now back
jokes.explanation: Good twist that recontextualizes earlier events

jokes.text: Carlton was right. It was only a three-and-a-half-hour virus.
jokes.setup: Will having ditched Jasmine claiming stomach problems
jokes.punchline: Jasmine's precise timing of Will's fake illness
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Jasmine caught on to Will's deception with specific timing

jokes.text: I hear they have nude beaches.
jokes.setup: Jasmine talking about romantic weekend plans
jokes.punchline: The visual implication of Jasmine at nude beaches
jokes.explanation: Cheap visual humor based on appearance rather than clever writing

jokes.text: I hear they have nude beaches.
jokes.setup: Jasmine talking about weekend plans
jokes.punchline: Her enthusiasm for nude beaches
jokes.explanation: Predictable escalation relying on appearance-based discomfort

jokes.text: Carlton!
jokes.setup: Will realizing Carlton is stuck with Jasmine for the weekend
jokes.punchline: Will's horrified realization and scream for Carlton
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that serves as episode button, economical and clear

tmdb_overview: In competition with Carlton, Will sets out to prove he's the best man for the job on Hilary's TV show by escorting a famous comedian's sister out on the town.
